title: pyrus hugh-mcallisteri
intro: Pyrus hugh-mcallisteri, also known as Hugh McAllister's pear, is a rare and endangered species of pear tree native to the western United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains a high concentration of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The medicinal properties of Pyrus hugh-mcallisteri are used to treat fever, pain, and inflammation, providing relief from headaches, arthritis, and other conditions. The bark can be prepared as a decoction or tincture for internal use, or as a poultice for topical application.
